finding: The strongest data-driven risk-prediction feature association was observed for rgc_graph_max_risk from the RGC graph/entity context group (absolute Spearman=0.5753).
Feature-set ablation shows that RGC-ExpandedContext achieved the highest mean macro-F1 among RGC feature variants.
The strongest monotonic driver of reward improvement was dt_base_delay_prob (absolute Spearman=0.8617).
The strongest monotonic driver of delay reduction was service_intensity (absolute Spearman=0.9530).
Local explanations were generated for 23 high-impact test decisions, showing row-level reward, risk, delay, service, and resilience differences.
The strongest stress-group response was observed under combined_stress, with average reward improvement of 38.76%.
